title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Inspector Claddock questions Emma regarding her activities on December 20th. Emma explains that she attended a church restoration fund meeting in Blakelockton, which concluded at 1:15 p.m., and subsequently had lunch with Mrs. Oudington and Miss Bartlett at the Cadina restaurant. Her afternoon was spent shopping in Greenford, Leesworth, and Bots, followed by tea at the Three Leaves Tea House at 5:15 p.m. She picked up Brian before returning home at 6:00 p.m. Emma also notes that her brothers, Alfred and Harold, arrived at the house on Saturday evening and the night before Christmas, respectively.

Claddock presents Emma with an empty envelope found outside the manor, which Emma identifies as her own handwriting from a letter she wrote to Matilda. This discovery suggests that the person in the coffin may indeed be Matilda. Meanwhile, Crocker receives a telegram from Amund Dessen confirming a yacht story involving Anna Stravinsky, who has arrived in Jamaica.

In a separate setting, Alexander and Lucy pack for a departure in a Rolls-Royce. Alexander discusses his father, Brian, a brave fighter pilot, and expresses a desire for Brian to find a stable family life. He suggests that Brian likes Lucy and implies he might be open to her becoming a stepmother.

Finally, Wetherell and Clutter review an investigation report concerning Harold’s alibi for December 20th. While Harold was seen at Sotheby's auction house at 3:30 p.m., he left shortly thereafter. His butler claims Harold returned home around 7:15 p.m. to prepare for a 7:30 p.m. gala, though the butler cannot confirm the exact time of arrival. Harold also attended a dinner at Cartel Hall but left before the speech concluded.
